registry.description: Dread is the deep, instinctive fear of something overwhelming — a fear that is not merely anxious but physically arresting, the kind that stops the body in its tracks. The Hebrew vocabulary overlaps with terror and fear but carries a specific note of awe-laden dread: the feeling of standing before power vastly greater than oneself. It appears in battle contexts (the dread that falls on enemies), in theophany contexts (the dread of encountering God), and in the experience of facing irreversible consequences.

terms.medium_def:
1) terror, dread
1a) dread
1b) object of dread

verses.esv_text: 1Ch 14:17 And the fame of David went out into all lands , and the Lord brought the fear of him upon all nations .
verses.preview_html: 1Ch 14:17 And the fame of David went out into all lands, and the Lord brought the fear of him upon all nations.

verses.esv_text: 1Sa 11:7 He took a yoke of oxen and cut them in pieces and sent them throughout all the territory of Israel by the hand of the messengers , saying , “ Whoever does not come out after Saul and Samuel , so shall it be done to his oxen !” Then the dread of the Lord fell upon the people , and they came out as one man .
verses.preview_html: 1Sa 11:7 He took a yoke of oxen and cut them in pieces and sent them throughout all the territory of Israel by the hand of the messengers, saying, “Whoever does not come out after Saul and Samuel, so shall it be done to his oxen!” Then the dread of the Lord fell upon the people, and they came out as one man.

verses.esv_text: Est 3:12 Then the king’s scribes were summoned on the thirteenth day of the first month , and an edict, according to all that Haman commanded , was written to the king’s satraps and to the governors over all the provinces and to the officials of all the peoples , to every province in its own script and every people in its own language . It was written in the name of King Ahasuerus and sealed with the king’s signet ring .

verses.esv_text: Est 8:9 The king’s scribes were summoned at that time , in the third month , which is the month of Sivan , on the twenty-third day. And an edict was written , according to all that Mordecai commanded concerning the Jews , to the satraps and the governors and the officials of the provinces from India to Ethiopia , 127 provinces , to each province in its own script and to each people in its own language , and also to the Jews in their script and their language .

verses.esv_text: Neh 5:15 The former governors who were before me laid heavy burdens on the people and took from them for their daily ration forty shekels of silver . Even their servants lorded it over the people . But I did not do so , because of the fear of God .
verses.preview_html: Neh 5:15 The former governors who were before me laid heavy burdens on the people and took from them for their daily ration forty shekels of silver. Even their servants lorded it over the people. But I did not do so, because of the fear of God.

verses.esv_text: Neh 5:18 Now what was prepared at my expense for each day was one ox and six choice sheep and birds , and every ten days all kinds of wine in abundance . Yet for all this I did not demand the food allowance of the governor , because the service was too heavy on this people .
verses.preview_html: Neh 5:18 Now what was prepared at my expense for each day was one ox and six choice sheep and birds, and every ten days all kinds of wine in abundance. Yet for all this I did not demand the food allowance of the governor, because the service was too heavy on this people.

terms.medium_def:
1) to fear, tremble, revere, dread, be in awe or dread
1a) (Qal)
1a1) to be in dread
1a2) to be in awe
1b) (Piel) to be in great dread
1c) (Hiphil) to cause to dread

verses.esv_text: Deu 28:66 Your life shall hang in doubt before you. Night and day you shall be in dread and have no assurance of your life .
verses.preview_html: Deu 28:66 Your life shall hang in doubt before you. Night and day you shall be in dread and have no assurance of your life.

verses.esv_text: Deu 28:67 In the morning you shall say , ‘ If only it were evening !’ and at evening you shall say , ‘ If only it were morning !’ because of the dread that your heart shall feel , and the sights that your eyes shall see .
verses.preview_html: Deu 28:67 In the morning you shall say, ‘If only it were evening!’ and at evening you shall say, ‘If only it were morning!’ because of the dread that your heart shall feel, and the sights that your eyes shall see.

verses.esv_text: Isa 12:2 “ Behold , God is my salvation ; I will trust , and will not be afraid ; for the Lord God is my strength and my song , and he has become my salvation .”
verses.preview_html:
Isa 12:2 “Behold, God is my salvation;
I will trust, and will not be afraid;
for the Lord God is my strength and my song,
and he has become my salvation.”

verses.esv_text: Isa 19:16 In that day the Egyptians will be like women , and tremble with fear before the hand that the Lord of hosts shakes over them .
verses.preview_html: Isa 19:16 In that day the Egyptians will be like women, and tremble with fear before the hand that the Lord of hosts shakes over them.

verses.esv_text: Isa 19:17 And the land of Judah will become a terror to the Egyptians . Everyone to whom it is mentioned will fear because of the purpose that the Lord of hosts has purposed against them .
verses.preview_html: Isa 19:17 And the land of Judah will become a terror to the Egyptians. Everyone to whom it is mentioned will fear because of the purpose that the Lord of hosts has purposed against them.

verses.esv_text: Isa 44:11 Behold , all his companions shall be put to shame , and the craftsmen are only human . Let them all assemble , let them stand forth . They shall be terrified ; they shall be put to shame together .
verses.preview_html: Isa 44:11 Behold, all his companions shall be put to shame, and the craftsmen are only human. Let them all assemble, let them stand forth. They shall be terrified; they shall be put to shame together.

verses.esv_text: Isa 44:8 Fear not , nor be afraid ; have I not told you from of old and declared it? And you are my witnesses ! Is there a God besides me? There is no Rock ; I know not any.”

Isa 44:8 Fear not, nor be afraid;
have I not told you from of old and declared it?
And you are my witnesses!

verses.esv_text: Jer 2:19 Your evil will chastise you, and your apostasy will reprove you. Know and see that it is evil and bitter for you to forsake the Lord your God ; the fear of me is not in you, declares the Lord God of hosts .
verses.preview_html:
Jer 2:19 Your evil will chastise you,
and your apostasy will reprove you.
Know and see that it is evil and bitter
for you to forsake the Lord your God;
the fear of me is not in you,

declares the Lord God of hosts.

terms.medium_def:
1) to tremble, dread, fear, oppress, prevail, break, be terrified, cause to tremble
1a) (Qal)
1a1) to cause to tremble, terrify
1b2) to tremble, feel dread
1b) (Niphal) to be awesome, be terrible
1c) (Hiphil)
1c1) to regard or treat with awe, regard or treat as awful
1c2) to inspire with awe, terrify

terms.medium_def:
1) to be grieved, loathe, abhor, feel a loathing or abhorrence or sickening dread
1a) (Qal)
1a1) to feel a loathing at, abhor
1a2) to feel a sickening dread
1b) (Hiphil)
1b1) to cause sickening dread
1b2) to cause loathing

terms.medium_def:
1) to loathe, be grieved, feel a loathing
1a) (Qal) to feel a loathing
1b) (Niphal)
1b1) to feel loathing against self
1b2) to detest
1c) (Hithpolel) to loathe oneself
Also means: na.qat (נָקַט "to tire" H5354)

verses.esv_text: Eze 20:43 And there you shall remember your ways and all your deeds with which you have defiled yourselves, and you shall loathe yourselves for all the evils that you have committed .
verses.preview_html: Eze 20:43 And there you shall remember your ways and all your deeds with which you have defiled yourselves, and you shall loathe yourselves for all the evils that you have committed.

verses.esv_text: Eze 36:31 Then you will remember your evil ways , and your deeds that were not good , and you will loathe yourselves for your iniquities and your abominations .
verses.preview_html: Eze 36:31 Then you will remember your evil ways, and your deeds that were not good, and you will loathe yourselves for your iniquities and your abominations.

verses.esv_text: Eze 6:9 then those of you who escape will remember me among the nations where they are carried captive , how I have been broken over their whoring heart that has departed from me and over their eyes that go whoring after their idols . And they will be loathsome in their own sight for the evils that they have committed , for all their abominations .
verses.preview_html: Eze 6:9 then those of you who escape will remember me among the nations where they are carried captive, how I have been broken over their whoring heart that has departed from me and over their eyes that go whoring after their idols. And they will be loathsome in their own sight for the evils that they have committed, for all their abominations.

verses.esv_text: Psa 119:158 I look at the faithless with disgust , because they do not keep your commands .
verses.preview_html:
Psa 119:158 I look at the faithless with disgust,
because they do not keep your commands.

verses.esv_text: Psa 139:21 Do I not hate those who hate you, O Lord ? And do I not loathe those who rise up against you?
verses.preview_html:
Psa 139:21 Do I not hate those who hate you, O Lord?
And do I not loathe those who rise up against you?

verses.esv_text: Psa 95:10 For forty years I loathed that generation and said , “They are a people who go astray in their heart , and they have not known my ways .”
verses.preview_html:
Psa 95:10 For forty years I loathed that generation
and said, “They are a people who go astray in their heart,
and they have not known my ways.”

verses.esv_text: Deu 28:60 And he will bring upon you again all the diseases of Egypt , of which you were afraid , and they shall cling to you .
verses.preview_html: Deu 28:60 And he will bring upon you again all the diseases of Egypt, of which you were afraid, and they shall cling to you.

verses.esv_text: Deu 9:19 For I was afraid of the anger and hot displeasure that the Lord bore against you, so that he was ready to destroy you. But the Lord listened to me that time also.
verses.preview_html: Deu 9:19 For I was afraid of the anger and hot displeasure that the Lord bore against you, so that he was ready to destroy you. But the Lord listened to me that time also.

verses.esv_text: Job 3:25 For the thing that I fear comes upon me, and what I dread befalls me .
verses.preview_html:
Job 3:25 For the thing that I fear comes upon me,
and what I dread befalls me.

verses.esv_text: Job 9:28 I become afraid of all my suffering, for I know you will not hold me innocent .
verses.preview_html:
Job 9:28 I become afraid of all my suffering,
for I know you will not hold me innocent.

verses.esv_text: Psa 119:39 Turn away the reproach that I dread , for your rules are good .

verses.esv_text: Jer 22:25 and give you into the hand of those who seek your life , into the hand of those of whom you are afraid , even into the hand of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon and into the hand of the Chaldeans .
verses.preview_html: Jer 22:25 and give you into the hand of those who seek your life, into the hand of those of whom you are afraid, even into the hand of Nebuchadnezzar king of Babylon and into the hand of the Chaldeans.

verses.esv_text: Jer 39:17 But I will deliver you on that day , declares the Lord , and you shall not be given into the hand of the men of whom you are afraid .
verses.preview_html: Jer 39:17 But I will deliver you on that day, declares the Lord, and you shall not be given into the hand of the men of whom you are afraid.
